meta.name: 10 Mile Intermediate
meta.subtitle: Break 90 minutes over 16 weeks
meta.description: For runners with a solid 10k base who want to break 90 minutes for 10 miles. Five running days per week, threshold-focused, with long runs building to 22km.
meta.coach_notes: Sub-90 for 10 miles is 5:35/km (8:59/mile). The threshold runs on Thursday develop exactly this pace. The long runs on Sunday build the endurance to sustain it through to the finish.

weeks.title: Base week 1
weeks.note: Opening week. Establish your easy pace and threshold feel. Nothing maximal.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — The first session of your plan. Run at a comfortable, conversational pace — slower than you think you need to. Building the habit matters more than the pace right now.

2km WU · 6x800m @ 10mi pace (90sec rec) · 2km CD — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Goblet squat 2x12 · Romanian deadlift 2x12 · Reverse lunge 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x30s — Your first lower body strength session. Keep loads light — this week is about learning the movements. Lower body strength is the foundation of running economy.

2km WU · 22min @ threshold (5:15–5:25/km) · 2km CD — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Pull-ups 2x12 · DB row 2x12 · Push-ups 2x12 · Face pulls 2x15 · Core circuit 2x30s — Your first upper body session. Focus on form, not load. Upper body and core strength directly improves your running posture and arm drive efficiency.
9km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
14km easy · 5:55–6:20/km — The first long run sets your aerobic foundation. Keep the pace genuinely easy — you should be able to hold a conversation throughout. Finishing comfortably is the goal.

weeks.title: Base week 2
weeks.note: Second week. Tempo run extends to 25 minutes.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 5x1km @ 10mi pace (2min rec) · 2km CD — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Back squat 3x12 · Romanian deadlift 3x12 · Bulgarian split squat 3x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 3x12s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

2km WU · 25min @ threshold (5:12–5:22/km) · 2km CD — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Pull-ups 3x12 · DB row 3x12 · Overhead press 3x12 · Face pulls 3x12 · Core circuit 3x12s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.
10km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
16km easy · 5:55–6:20/km — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.
9km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Pull-ups 3x12 · DB row 3x12 · Overhead press 3x12 · Face pulls 3x12 · Core circuit 3x12s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.
2km WU · 28min @ threshold · 2km CD — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Back squat 3x12 · Romanian deadlift 3x12 · Bulgarian split squat 3x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 3x12s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.
10km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
18km easy · 5:55–6:20/km — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Deload week
weeks.note: Deliberate recovery week. Volume drops by 35%.  Rest well.

sessions.det: 7km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
7km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
6km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
6km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
13km easy · Comfortable and relaxed — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

weeks.note: Deliberate recovery week. Volume drops by 35%. You are now in the race-specific phase. Rest well.

weeks.title: Race specific 1
weeks.note: Race-pace intervals. Six reps at exactly 5:35/km.

sessions.det: 9km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km WU · 6x1km @ 5:35/km (2min rec) · 2km CD — Peak-phase intervals — this is where your fitness is tested. These are hard but controlled. If the pace slips, the session is done.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 4x5 · Core circuit 4x5s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.
2km WU · 35min @ 5:10–5:20/km · 2km CD — Threshold work at peak phase. Your lactate threshold determines the pace you can sustain for an hour. These sessions push that ceiling up.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 4x5 · Core circuit 4x5s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.
10km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

17km easy · final 3km at race pace — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.note: Long intervals at race pace. Confidence builds.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 4x5 · Core circuit 4x5s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.
2km WU · 30min @ 5:08–5:18/km · 2km CD — Threshold work at peak phase. Your lactate threshold determines the pace you can sustain for an hour. These sessions push that ceiling up.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 4x5 · Core circuit 4x5s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.
10km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

22km · First 16km easy · Final 6km at race pace — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Race specific 3
weeks.note: Peak week. The 14km at race pace on Sunday is the closest you get to racing before race day.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 4x5 · Core circuit 4x5s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.
10km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

7km easy · 14km at race pace · Treat as a long race-pace run — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Taper prep
weeks.note: Volume begins to reduce. Intensity stays.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 6x800m @ slightly faster than race pace (90sec rec) · 2km CD — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Back squat 3x12 · Romanian deadlift 3x12 · Bulgarian split squat 3x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 3x12s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

Pull-ups 3x12 · DB row 3x12 · Overhead press 3x12 · Face pulls 3x12 · Core circuit 3x12s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.
9km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
18km easy — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Taper 1
weeks.note: Taper begins. Volume drops 35%. Intensity maintained.

sessions.det: 7km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

2km WU · 5x800m @ race pace (2min rec) · 2km CD — Sharp intervals close to race week keep your legs feeling quick without adding volume. The fitness is already built — this is a reminder, not a workout.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
2km WU · 18min threshold · 2km CD — A short threshold session to maintain the feel of race effort. Comfortably hard — not racing.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
7km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
13km easy · Comfortable — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

weeks.title: Taper 2
weeks.note: Final taper week. Volume light, intensity maintained for sharpness.

sessions.det: 5km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

2km WU · 4x800m @ race pace (2min rec) · 2km CD — Sharp intervals close to race week keep your legs feeling quick without adding volume. The fitness is already built — this is a reminder, not a workout.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

5km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
9km easy · Stay fresh — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

1.5km WU · Strides · Race: through 5 miles at 5:35/km, build final 2 miles · You have trained for this distance — trust it

nut.d: Porridge with banana or toast with peanut butter. 500ml water. Only familiar foods.
200ml water. Warm up well — 15min easy, strides. For 10-mile and half, a proper warm-up is essential.
Water at every station. For 10-mile: gel at 6–7 miles if trained with gels. For half: gel at miles 6 and 10.
Water immediately. 500ml in 10 minutes. Banana or protein snack. Full meal within 90 minutes.
Proper meal with protein, carbohydrates, and whatever you have been looking forward to. Well earned.
